name: D-branes (Polchinski)
summary: D-branes are extended objects in string theory defined by mixed Dirichlet-Neumann boundary conditions on open-string endpoints. Polchinski (1995) showed they carry a complete set of electric and magnetic Ramond-Ramond charges, are intrinsic to type II string theory, and are required by string duality. Provided the long-missing carriers of RR charges and revolutionised string theory by enabling explicit microscopic constructions of black-hole states (Strominger-Vafa), gauge/gravity duality (AdS/CFT), and braneworld phenomenology.

addresses_puzzles.note: Provides the underlying microscopic objects (D-brane bound states) whose quantum-mechanical degeneracy reproduces the Bekenstein-Hawking entropy in Strominger-Vafa-type constructions. Foundational for the AdS/CFT-based resolution of BH information paradox in asymptotically-AdS settings.
D-brane constructions of the SM (Standard Model on intersecting / magnetised branes) provide a string-theoretic UV completion in which the EW hierarchy is fixed by compactification geometry.
D-branes extend perturbative string theory to include non-perturbative solitonic states; essential ingredient of any complete string-TOE but not the framework by itself (Polchinski 1995).

touches_benchmarks.note: D-brane SM constructions typically predict dimension-6 proton decay rates near Super-K bounds for natural compactification volumes.

new_symmetries: worldvolume gauge symmetry on each D-brane stack
introduces.note: Compactification choice, brane configuration, fluxes, and string coupling determine the 4D phenomenology.

history: Polchinski (1995), 'Dirichlet-Branes and Ramond-Ramond Charges', Phys. Rev. Lett. 75, 4724, arXiv:hep-th/9510017 — established D-branes as the carriers of RR charges. Triggered the second superstring revolution; underlies AdS/CFT (Maldacena 1997), explicit black-hole microstate counting (Strominger-Vafa 1996), brane-world phenomenology (RS, ADD), and the modern string-landscape program.

references.citation: Polchinski, 'Dirichlet-Branes and Ramond-Ramond Charges', Phys. Rev. Lett. 75, 4724 (1995)
